title: Micronaut
description: Micronaut framework: compile-time DI, HTTP server and clients, AOP, data access, resilience and GraalVM.
questions: What is Micronaut and what problems with traditional JVM frameworks (like classic Spring) was it designed to solve? Name its three headline characteristics.

Micronaut performs dependency injection at compile time using annotation processors. Explain what that means concretely — what is generated during compilation, and what does the framework NOT have to do at runtime compared to a reflection-based container?
Walk through the main bean scopes in Micronaut: `@Singleton`, `@Prototype`, `@RequestScope` and `@Context`. When is a `@Singleton` bean actually created, and how does `@Context` change that?

How do you define beans for classes you don't own (e.g. from a third-party library)? Explain `@Factory` classes and `@Bean` methods, and how injection points (constructor vs field) work with `@Inject`.

What does the `@Requires` annotation do? Give three different kinds of conditions it supports (e.g. property, class on classpath, environment) and explain how conditional beans enable Micronaut's modular configuration.

Compare the ways to consume configuration in Micronaut: `@Value`, `@Property` and `@ConfigurationProperties` classes. When would you choose type-safe configuration binding, and how does it get validated?
How do environments work in Micronaut (e.g. `dev`, `test`, `cloud`)? How are environment-specific configuration files resolved, and how does `@Requires(env = ...)` interact with them?

Describe how an HTTP endpoint is built in Micronaut: `@Controller`, method annotations like `@Get`/`@Post`, and argument binding (path variables, query params, request body). How does routing differ from Spring MVC's runtime handler mapping?

What is a declarative HTTP client (`@Client` on an interface)? Explain what Micronaut generates for it, how it integrates with service discovery via service IDs, and why this beats hand-writing HTTP calls.
Explain server and client filters (`HttpServerFilter` / `HttpClientFilter`): what are they for, how do you control their order and the paths they apply to, and what are typical use cases?
How does error handling work in Micronaut controllers? Compare local and global `@Error` handlers and `ExceptionHandler` beans — when would you use each?
How does validation work in Micronaut (`@Valid`, `@Validated`, jakarta.validation annotations)? What part of the validation support is done at compile time, and what does that buy you?

Explain AOP in Micronaut: how do you write a `MethodInterceptor` with an `@Around` annotation, and how do Micronaut's compile-time proxies differ from Spring's runtime JDK/CGLIB proxies (including the self-invocation problem)?

What is Micronaut Data and how does it differ fundamentally from Spring Data? Explain what happens to a repository interface method like `findByNameAndAgeGreaterThan` at compile time, and why that helps both performance and correctness.

Micronaut's HTTP server is built on Netty's event loop. Why must you never block the event loop, how do you mark blocking work with `@ExecuteOn(TaskExecutors.BLOCKING)`, and what happens when a controller returns a reactive type like `Mono`/`Publisher`?
Describe Micronaut's built-in resilience features: `@Retryable`, `@CircuitBreaker` and `@Fallback`. How does each behave, and where would you apply them in a microservice calling other services?
Why is Micronaut particularly well suited to GraalVM native image? Explain what reflection-heavy frameworks struggle with under native image, and what Micronaut's ahead-of-time approach avoids.

What does `@Introspected` do? Explain bean introspection: why Micronaut needs its own reflection-free way to read/write bean properties, and where it is used (serialization, validation, Micronaut Data).

How do you test a Micronaut application? Explain `@MicronautTest` (what it starts, how injection works in tests), replacing collaborators with `@MockBean`, and testing a controller through the embedded server with an injected `@Client`.
What do Micronaut's management endpoints provide (health, metrics, refresh, etc.)? How do you enable and secure them, and how does Micronaut integrate with Micrometer for metrics export?

How does Micronaut integrate with Kafka and RabbitMQ (`@KafkaListener`, `@KafkaClient`, `@RabbitListener`)? Explain what the annotation processor generates, how consumer concurrency and error handling are configured, and how you would test messaging code with Testcontainers.

How does transaction management work in Micronaut with Micronaut Data and JDBC? Explain `@Transactional` (jakarta vs Micronaut's own), how it decides commit vs rollback on exceptions, how it composes with `@KafkaListener` consumers, and why there is no self-invocation proxy problem like in Spring.
